steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must show that Ecuador has different types of volcanoes. The correct choice must pair two Ecuadorian volcanoes that are classified as different types in the table.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: includes Alcedo, which is in the Galápagos rather than mainland Ecuador, so it does not serve as a valid Ecuadorian example. ✗
  • Choice B: misidentifies Cuicocha's type, so the pairing is factually wrong per the table. ✗
  • Choice C: also includes Alcedo or misclassifies a volcano type, making the example inaccurate. ✗
  • Choice D — Cuicocha (caldera) and Cayambe (compound): both are Ecuadorian volcanoes and are classified as different types in the table — exactly what the student needs to demonstrate. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
